Title: Phylogeny-Aware Chemoinformatic Analysis of Chemical Diversity in Lamiaceae Enables Iridoid Pathway Assembly and Discovery of Aucubin Synthase

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ancestral aucubin/catalpol route (Damtoft-Jensen route II-related branch)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream oxidation step in aucubin branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces aucubin from bartsioside in microsome assays, identifying the enzyme for a predicted aucubin-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related specialized metabolite; iridoids act on generalist herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected by coexpression with early iridoid pathway genes across young and mature leaves, stems, petioles, roots, closed buds, open flowers, and fruit transcriptomes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed with early iridoid biosynthetic genes in C. americana transcriptome
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gal10 promoter in yeast heterologous expression system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression-based candidate selection; orthogroup/PFAM annotation; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae microsomes; substrate feeding with bartsioside; LC-MS/XIC product detection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro microsome incubation with NADPH at 30 °C in the dark
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq for C. americana transcriptome/expression from published data (Hamilton et al. 2020); orthogroup analysis; no accession specified in main text for transcriptome here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for assembling and engineering aucubin/catalpol iridoid pathways in heterologous hosts or plants
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Artemisia annua cytochrome P450 reductase (heterologous coexpression partner)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Calam.02G160400.1 from C. americana was identified from a CYP72 orthogroup enriched among iridoid-pathway coexpressed genes. 2. Microsomes expressing this enzyme converted bartsioside to aucubin. 3. The enzyme validates a pathway step predicted by the phylogeny-aware model even though neither metabolite had been reported in Callicarpa.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Renamed Callicarpa americana aucubin synthase (CaAS); one of three C. americana CYP72 candidates in orthogroup OG0001031.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream oxidation step in aucubin branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ortholog catalyzes the predicted bartsioside-to-aucubin conversion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related specialized metabolite; iridoids act on generalist herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gal10 promoter in yeast heterologous expression system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic ortholog selection; heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; substrate feeding; LC-MS/XIC product detection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro microsome incubation with NADPH at 30 °C in the dark
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence from transcriptome/orthogroup analysis; accession in main text not otherwise provided beyond transcript ID and GenBank
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports broader deployment of aucubin synthase orthologs for pathway engineering across Lamiaceae
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Artemisia annua cytochrome P450 reductase (heterologous coexpression partner)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A V. agnus-castus CYP72 ortholog from the CaAS-containing clade was selected for testing. 2. The enzyme converted bartsioside to aucubin in microsomes. 3. This supports conservation of the aucubin-forming step within the predicted ancestral pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named Vitex agnus-castus aucubin synthase (VaAS); tested because Vitex genus includes the only bartsioside report in the curated data set.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream oxidation step in aucubin branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Outgroup ortholog catalyzes the predicted bartsioside-to-aucubin conversion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related specialized metabolite; iridoids act on generalist herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gal10 promoter in yeast heterologous expression system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic ortholog selection; heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; substrate feeding; LC-MS/XIC product detection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro microsome incubation with NADPH at 30 °C in the dark
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence from transcriptome/orthogroup analysis; accession in main text not otherwise provided beyond transcript ID and GenBank
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides an outgroup aucubin synthase useful for comparative biochemistry and pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Artemisia annua cytochrome P450 reductase (heterologous coexpression partner)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A P. tomentosa ortholog from the same CYP72 clade was tested as an outgroup enzyme. 2. The enzyme converted bartsioside to aucubin in microsomes. 3. Its activity strengthens the hypothesis that this reaction was present in the ancestral pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named Paulownia tomentosa aucubin synthase (PtAS); outgroup validation strengthened ancestral-pathway inference.
